 {"id":"bd-1323","title":"E2E Tests: Dashboard visual regression with VHS recordings","description":"## Overview
End-to-end visual regression tests using VHS (terminal recording) to verify the dashboard renders correctly across different scenarios.

## Prerequisites
- VHS installed: \`brew install vhs\` or \`go install github.com/charmbracelet/vhs@latest\`
- ffmpeg for video processing
- ImageMagick for screenshot comparison

## Test File Location
- tests/e2e/dashboard_visual_test.go
- testdata/vhs/*.tape (VHS tape files)
- testdata/golden/*.png (golden screenshots)

## VHS Tape Files

### 1. Basic Render Test
\`\`\`tape
# File: testdata/vhs/dashboard-basic.tape
Output testdata/vhs/dashboard-basic.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800
Set Theme "Catppuccin Mocha"
Set TypingSpeed 50ms

# Start dashboard with test session
Type "ntm dashboard test-session"
Enter
Sleep 3s

# Take screenshot after initial render
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/basic-initial.png

# Verify no duplication by checking status bar area
Sleep 1s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/basic-stable.png
\`\`\`

### 2. Resize Test
\`\`\`tape
# File: testdata/vhs/dashboard-resize.tape
Output testdata/vhs/dashboard-resize.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800
Set Theme "Catppuccin Mocha"

Type "ntm dashboard test-session"
Enter
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/resize-wide.png

# Narrow terminal
Set Width 800
Sleep 1s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/resize-narrow.png

# Very narrow
Set Width 600
Sleep 1s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/resize-compact.png

# Back to wide
Set Width 1600
Sleep 1s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/resize-ultrawide.png
\`\`\`

### 3. Navigation Test
\`\`\`tape
# File: testdata/vhs/dashboard-navigation.tape
Output testdata/vhs/dashboard-navigation.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800

Type "ntm dashboard test-session"
Enter
Sleep 2s

# Navigate down
Type "j"
Sleep 300ms
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/nav-down1.png

Type "j"
Sleep 300ms
Type "j"
Sleep 300ms
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/nav-down3.png

# Navigate up
Type "k"
Sleep 300ms
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/nav-up.png

# Select by number
Type "5"
Sleep 500ms
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/nav-select5.png
\`\`\`

### 4. Refresh Test (No Accumulation)
\`\`\`tape
# File: testdata/vhs/dashboard-refresh.tape
Output testdata/vhs/dashboard-refresh.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800

Type "ntm dashboard test-session"
Enter
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/refresh-before.png

# Manual refresh
Type "r"
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/refresh-after1.png

Type "r"
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/refresh-after2.png

Type "r"
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/refresh-after3.png
\`\`\`

## Visual Comparison Script
\`\`\`bash
#!/bin/bash
# scripts/visual-regression.sh

set -euo pipefail

TESTDATA="testdata"
GOLDEN="$TESTDATA/golden"
SCREENSHOTS="$TESTDATA/screenshots"
THRESHOLD=100  # Maximum pixel difference allowed

echo "Running VHS tapes..."
for tape in $TESTDATA/vhs/*.tape; do
    echo "  Running: $tape"
    vhs "$tape" 2>&1 | tee -a vhs.log
done

echo "Comparing screenshots..."
FAILED=0
for golden in $GOLDEN/*.png; do
    name=$(basename "$golden")
    screenshot="$SCREENSHOTS/$name"
    
    if [[ ! -f "$screenshot" ]]; then
        echo "  MISSING: $name"
        FAILED=1
        continue
    fi
    
    # Compare with ImageMagick
    diff=$(compare -metric AE "$golden" "$screenshot" /dev/null 2>&1 || true)
    
    if [[ "$diff" -gt "$THRESHOLD" ]]; then
        echo "  FAIL: $name (diff: $diff pixels)"
        # Generate diff image for debugging
        compare "$golden" "$screenshot" "$TESTDATA/diff-$name" || true
        FAILED=1
    else
        echo "  PASS: $name (diff: $diff pixels)"
    fi
done

if [[ "$FAILED" -eq 1 ]]; then
    echo ""
    echo "Visual regression tests FAILED"
    echo "Check testdata/diff-*.png for differences"
    exit 1
fi

echo ""
echo "All visual regression tests PASSED"
\`\`\`

## E2E Test Wrapper
\`\`\`go
// tests/e2e/dashboard_visual_test.go
func TestDashboard_VisualRegression(t *testing.T) {
    if os.Getenv("RUN_VISUAL_TESTS") == "" {
        t.Skip("set RUN_VISUAL_TESTS=1 to run visual regression tests")
    }
    
    // Run visual regression script
    cmd := exec.Command("bash", "scripts/visual-regression.sh")
    cmd.Dir = projectRoot()
    output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
    
    t.Log(string(output))
    
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("visual regression failed: %v", err)
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Specific Assertions

### No Status Bar Duplication
Check that screenshots contain exactly one instance of status bar text.

### Proper Layout at All Sizes
- 80x24: Compact mode, essential elements visible
- 120x40: Standard mode, all panels visible
- 200x60: Wide mode, expanded layout

### Animation Stability
Multiple screenshots at same state should be visually identical (except for animations).

## CI Integration
\`\`\`yaml
visual-tests:
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - name: Install VHS
      run: |
        brew install vhs imagemagick ffmpeg
    - name: Run visual tests
      run: |
        RUN_VISUAL_TESTS=1 go test -v ./tests/e2e/...
    - name: Upload diff images
      if: failure()
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: visual-diffs
        path: testdata/diff-*.png
\`\`\`

## Updating Golden Files
\`\`\`bash
# After intentional UI changes, update golden files:
UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 RUN_VISUAL_TESTS=1 go test -v ./tests/e2e/...

# Or manually:
cp testdata/screenshots/*.png testdata/golden/
\`\`\`

## Logging
All test runs should log:
- VHS version
- Terminal size for each tape
- Screenshot file paths
- Pixel difference for each comparison
- Full command output on failure","notes":"VHS tape files created in testdata/vhs/. E2E test wrapper in tests/e2e/dashboard_visual_test.go. Visual regression script at scripts/visual-regression.sh. Directories testdata/golden and testdata/screenshots created. Golden images need to be generated when VHS is installed.  {"id":"bd-1370q","title":"EPIC: Support Bundle (Diagnostics + Safe Sharing)","description":"## Summary
Add a first-class way to generate **safe, shareable diagnostics** for NTM issues.

Today users often paste logs/pane output manually (high leak risk, low reproducibility). A support bundle command can capture:
- environment + tool versions
- effective config
- recent event log slice
- relevant session snapshot(s)
- optional pane excerpts
…while applying redaction and respecting privacy mode.

## Goals
- One command produces a single archive file (`.zip` or `.tar.gz`).
- Safe-by-default: redaction enabled by default; explicit overrides required.
- Deterministic manifest with checksums for integrity.

## Non-goals
- Uploading bundles to third-party services (keep local; users can share manually).

## Success Criteria
- Bundle generation works without running sessions (still includes env/config).
- Works in robot mode with stable JSON output.
- Includes enough info to reproduce common bugs (tmux base-index, config parsing, etc.).
- Comprehensive unit + E2E tests with detailed logs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:26:55.667286751Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:26:55.667286751Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-13rct","title":"Task: Implement EarlyStopDetector for marginal utility stopping","description":"# Task: EarlyStopDetector Implementation

Stop ensemble when new findings rate drops below threshold.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/early_stop.go
type EarlyStopConfig struct {
    MinAgentsBeforeStop int     // Run at least N agents first
    FindingsThreshold   float64 // Stop if findings/token < threshold
    SimilarityThreshold float64 // Stop if output similarity > threshold
    WindowSize          int     // Evaluate over last N agents
    Enabled             bool
}

type EarlyStopDetector struct {
    Config        EarlyStopConfig
    Outputs       []ModeOutput
    TokensSpent   []int
}

type StopDecision struct {
    ShouldStop    bool
    Reason        string
    FindingsRate  float64
    SimilarityScore float64
    AgentsRun     int
}

func NewEarlyStopDetector(cfg EarlyStopConfig) *EarlyStopDetector
func (d *EarlyStopDetector) RecordOutput(output ModeOutput, tokens int)
func (d *EarlyStopDetector) ShouldStop() StopDecision
func (d *EarlyStopDetector) CalculateFindingsRate() float64
func (d *EarlyStopDetector) CalculateSimilarity() float64
```

## Stopping Criteria

### Findings Rate Drop
```
Rate = (new unique findings in window) / (tokens spent in window)
Stop if: rate < threshold AND agents_run >= min_agents
```

### Output Similarity
```
Similarity = average pairwise similarity of outputs in window
Stop if: similarity > threshold (outputs are redundant)
```

## Logging
- Log each decision with metrics
- Log when approaching threshold
- Log final stop reason

- Wait up to 3 seconds for exit

2. Target format is session:1.N (window is 1, not 0)
3.  {"id":"bd-1r6go","title":"DCG: Implement PreToolUse hook configuration for Claude agents","description":"Auto-configure PreToolUse hook for Claude Code agents when spawning.

## Background

Claude Code supports hooks that run before tool execution:
```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "PreToolUse": [{
      "matcher": "Bash",
      "command": "dcg check --command \"\""
    }]
  }
}
```

When dcg check returns non-zero, the tool execution is blocked.

## Implementation

### When spawning Claude agent:
1. Check if DCG is enabled and available
2. Generate hook configuration JSON
3. Set hook via environment variable or config file
4. Verify hook is active after spawn

### Hook Configuration
The hook needs to:
- Match only Bash tool invocations
- Pass the full command to dcg
- Handle multi-line commands properly
- Preserve exit code semantics

## Code Location
- internal/integrations/dcg/hooks.go
- internal/spawn/claude.go (add hook configuration)

## Testing
- Spawn agent with DCG enabled, verify hook present
- Spawn agent with DCG disabled, verify no hook

 {"id":"bd-2xmz3","title":"E2E Tests: Redaction across send/copy/mail","description":"## Goal
Validate end-to-end that secrets do not leak via the most common user flows.

## Scenarios
1. `ntm send` (warn): prompt contains a fake OpenAI key → warning emitted, prompt delivered unchanged.
2. `ntm send` (redact): prompt contains fake key → pane receives redacted placeholder.
3. `ntm send` (block): prompt contains fake key → send fails unless `--allow-secret`.
4. `ntm copy`/`ntm save`: pane output contains fake key → redact/block behaviors verified.
   - Prefer `--output <file>` (avoid clipboard flakiness) and assert file contents.
5. `ntm mail send`: message contains fake key → redact/block behaviors verified.
   - Use a local `httptest` Agent Mail stub to capture the payload and assert no plaintext secret markers are persisted/sent.

## Test Harness + Logging Requirements
- Implement as Go E2E tests under `e2e/` with the existing build tag (`//go:build e2e`).
- Use the existing E2E helpers:
  - `e2e.NewTestSuite(t, scenario)`
  - `suite.Setup()`, `suite.SpawnAgent(...)`, `suite.SendPrompt(...)`, `suite.CaptureOutput(...)`
  - `e2e.TestLogger` for structured logs (already writes to `E2E_LOG_DIR` or `/tmp/ntm-e2e-logs`).
- Include a stable run identifier (`test_run_id`) in the scenario name and in log lines.
- Capture and log:
  - exact command lines executed
  - stdout/stderr (raw) for each step
  - robot JSON outputs (pretty printed via `logger.LogJSON`)
  - computed redaction summaries (counts by category)

## Fixtures
- Use synthetic secret fixtures only (never real keys):
  - e.g., `sk-FAKE_...`, `ghp_FAKE...`, JWT-like strings

## Acceptance Criteria
- Default unit suite remains green: `go test ./...`.
- E2E suite passes: `go test -tags e2e ./e2e`.
- Logs make it obvious what was redacted/blocked and where.
- Assertions verify plaintext secret markers never reach panes/files/mail when `redact`/`block` is enabled.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:23:33.526623787Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-02T03:14:10.241884254Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T03:14:10.241807841Z","close_reason":"Implemented E2E tests for redaction across send/copy. All tests passing: warn/redact/block modes for send, copy with redaction, multiple secrets detection. Fixed pre-existing privacy_test.go build issues.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xmz3","depends_on_id":"bd-1kbjc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:23:33.557126405Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xmz3","depends_on_id":"bd-1sjqs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:23:33.539476636Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xmz3","depends_on_id":"bd-351ir","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:23:33.548251502Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xmz3","depends_on_id":"bd-3a7yx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:23:33.566034739Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf"}]}

## Component Overview
A Bubble Tea component that displays reasoning modes assigned to each window in the ensemble, including tier chips.

## Visual Design
```
┌─ Ensemble: arch-review ─────────────────────────────┐
│                                                      │
│  Reasoning Modes                                     │
│  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━                                     │

│  [0] F1 Argument Mapping  [CORE]  claude  ████████░░ │
│  [1] E2 Root Cause        [CORE]  codex   ██████░░░░ │
│  [2] F43 Systems Thinking [ADV]   gemini  ██████████ │
│  [3] L3 Meta-cognitive    [CORE]  claude  ░░░░░░░░░░ │

│                                                      │
│  Progress: 2/5 complete                              │
│  Strategy: adversarial                               │
│                                                      │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

// internal/tui/ensemble_modes.go

}

- Agent type in subtle color

- Keyboard navigation between modes

 {"id":"bd-2yi1i","title":"Task: Support bundle privacy-mode enforcement","description":"## Scope
Ensure `ntm support-bundle` respects Privacy Mode semantics when bundling session data.

## Why
Support bundles are intended for safe sharing. Privacy Mode is intended to avoid persistence. The combination must be explicit and unsurprising.

## Required Behavior
- If the target session is privacy-enabled:
  - default behavior must **exclude** any content that could contain user text (pane excerpts, prompt history slices, raw event bodies).
  - allow inclusion only with an explicit, scary override (e.g., `--allow-persist` or a dedicated `--allow-private-content`).
- If privacy mode is enabled globally by default (per spec), the same rules apply.
- The bundle should still be useful in privacy mode:
  - doctor/version/config metadata is OK
  - include redaction summaries if redaction is enabled

## Integration Points
- Reuse the centralized privacy gating decision (bd-26364) rather than re-implementing ad-hoc checks.
- Ensure robot output (if used) reports:
  - whether privacy mode was detected
  - whether private content was included or suppressed
  - any warnings emitted

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bundle generation in privacy mode is safe-by-default and does not silently persist user text.
- CLI prints clear messaging when content was suppressed and how to override.
- E2E coverage:
  - ensure the privacy-mode scenario in bd-fgnlw passes (suppressed by default; allowed only with explicit override).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T04:14:30.215185013Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T02:46:10.360511682Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T02:46:10.360370990Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2yi1i","depends_on_id":"bd-1a542","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T04:14:30.229282425Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2yi1i","depends_on_id":"bd-26364","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T04:14:30.236233494Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2yi1i","depends_on_id":"bd-5h1bi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T04:14:30.221836835Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ywo","title":"Add default prompts per agent type - --cc-default-prompt, --cod-default-prompt, --gmi-default-prompt in config","description":"Add default prompts per agent type - configure cc-default-prompt, cod-default-prompt, gmi-default-prompt in config.

## Problem Being Solved

Different agent types benefit from different default prompts. Currently no way to set per-type defaults.

## Config Schema

```toml
[prompts]
cc_default = "You are a Claude Code agent. Focus on code quality."
cod_default = "You are a Codex agent. Be concise and direct."
gmi_default = "You are a Gemini agent. Consider multiple approaches."

# Or use files
cc_default_file = "~/.config/ntm/prompts/cc_default.md"
cod_default_file = "~/.config/ntm/prompts/cod_default.md"
gmi_default_file = "~/.config/ntm/prompts/gmi_default.md"
```

## Behavior
1. On spawn, load default prompt for each agent type
2. Prepend to any user-provided prompt
3. Skip if agent type has no default configured

## CLI Override
```bash
ntm spawn my_session --cc=2 --cc-prompt "Override default"
```

## Robot Mode API
ntm --robot-default-prompts

Returns JSON:
```json
{
  "cc_default": "You are a Claude...",
  "cod_default": "...",
  "gmi_default": "..."
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Config supports cc_default, cod_default, gmi_default strings
2. Config supports cc_default_file, etc. for file-based prompts
3. Default prompts prepended to spawn prompts
4. CLI flags override config defaults
5. Empty default = no prepend
6. Robot mode returns current defaults

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/config/prompts_test.go
- Test loading from string vs file
- Test override priority

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# [E2E-DEFAULT-PROMPTS] Default Prompts Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-DEFAULT-PROMPTS]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing default prompts"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. Config supports --cc-default-prompt in [agents.defaults]
2. Config supports --cod-default-prompt in [agents.defaults]
3. Config supports --gmi-default-prompt in [agents.defaults]
4. Default prompts applied on spawn
5. Command line overrides config
6. Unit tests for config parsing
7. E2E test verifying defaults applied","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-308f5","title":"rano Integration: Network Observer for Per-Agent API Tracking","description":"Integrate rano for network-level visibility into agent API calls.

## Project Location
/dp/rano (Rust CLI)

## Problem Being Solved

When agents are working:
- Which agent is making API calls?
- What is the latency per call?
- How much data is being transferred?
- Which provider is being called?

Currently invisible without rano.

## What rano Does

rano monitors network activity per process:

### Data Collection
- Reads /proc/net/tcp, /proc/net/tcp6
- Correlates connections to PIDs via /proc/<pid>/fd
- Tracks bytes sent/received per connection
- Attributes to known providers (api.anthropic.com, api.openai.com, etc.)

### Key rano Commands
```bash
rano status              # Current connections
rano status --pid 1234   # Connections for specific PID
rano history --today     # Historical data
rano watch               # Real-time monitoring
```

## Integration Strategy for NTM

### 1. PID Mapping
NTM knows which PID belongs to which pane via tmux session → pane → shell PID.

### 2. Dashboard Widget
Show per-agent network activity.

### 3. Robot Mode Commands
- --robot-rano-stats: Get network stats per agent

## Acceptance Criteria

1. rano binary detected and version checked
2. PID-to-pane mapping implemented
3. Per-agent traffic attribution works
4. Provider breakdown accurate
5. Config section [integrations.rano] works
6. Graceful fallback when rano missing
7. Unit tests for detection and attribution (80% coverage)
8. E2E test for full rano integration","acceptance_criteria":"1. rano binary detected and version checked
2. PID-to-pane mapping implemented
3. Per-agent traffic attribution works
4. Provider breakdown accurate
5. Config section [integrations.rano] works
6. Graceful fallback when rano missing
7. Unit tests for detection and attribution
8. E2E test for full rano integration","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:13:42.503613234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:10:14.654483967Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-308f5","depends_on_id":"bd-99a2i","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-30nv1","title":"Implement keystroke_echo probe method","description":"Implement keystroke_echo probe method.

## Method

Send invisible character, check for cursor/output response.

## Implementation

```go
func probeKeystrokeEcho(session string, pane int, timeout time.Duration) ProbeResult {
    // 1. Capture baseline (include cursor position if possible)
    baseline := capturePaneContent(session, pane)
    
    // 2. Send null byte (invisible, non-disruptive)
    exec.Command("/usr/bin/tmux", "send-keys", "-t", target, "\x00").Run()
    
    // 3. Wait for response (poll with short intervals)
    deadline := time.Now().Add(timeout)
    for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
        current := capturePaneContent(session, pane)
        if current != baseline {
            return ProbeResult{
                Responsive: true,
                LatencyMs: ...,
            }
        }
        time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)
    }
    
    // 4. No change detected
    return ProbeResult{Responsive: false}
}
```

## Considerations

- Null byte may not echo in all shells
- Alternative: Send ESC sequence that triggers cursor report

 {"id":"bd-30o1y","title":"Unit Tests: ntm assign --clear assignment removal logic","description":"## Test Coverage
Unit tests for ntm assign --clear functionality.

## Test Cases

### Single Clear
- Clear assigned bead
- Verify assignment removed from store
- Verify file reservations released

### Batch Clear
- Clear multiple beads (comma-separated)
- Verify all cleared correctly
- Partial failure handling

### Clear by Pane
- Clear all assignments for pane 3
- Handle pane with no assignments

### Clear Failed
- Clear only failed assignments
- Leave working/completed untouched

### Error Handling
- Clear non-assigned bead -> error
- Clear completed bead without --force -> error
- Clear completed bead with --force -> success

### Edge Cases
- Clear already cleared assignment
- Clear during concurrent assignment

## Test File

 {"id":"bd-30ws","title":"Task: Implement SummarizeSession() function","description":"## Purpose
Generate concise, actionable summaries of agent session output at session end.

## Scope
- Implement SummarizeSession() in internal/summary/generator.go
- Extract key accomplishments, changes made, and pending work
- Parse structured output from agent sessions
- Support multiple summary formats (brief, detailed, handoff)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Summaries capture all significant changes
- File changes are listed with context
- Pending work items are extracted
- Error summary included if applicable
- Token count reasonable for injection into next session

## Technical Notes
- Consider using LLM for summarization if available
- Fallback to pattern-based extraction
- Integrate with Agent Mail for thread summaries","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
